brief_justification: Arrives at exact correct answer (15M→16M liters) with valid arithmetic; explains Jevons paradox mechanism and why elasticity>1 causes backfire, going beyond surface calculation; proceeds linearly without naming assumptions, potential biases, or reflecting on method; runs calculation once with no counterfactual check or boundary test (e.g., what elasticity value is the break-even); states conclusion confidently without flagging assumptions in the elasticity model or uncertainty in its application; considers no alternatives (e.g., partial rebound, fleet size changes, market constraints); every step is explicit and a third party could fully reproduce the derivation from the response alone.
